title: Week 3: Python Basics III
text:
Project 3: Random Number Guesser

The TaskThe Solution


Your task is to code the number guesser game. You should generate a random number and the user has to keep guessing until they find the number.
To do:
Initialize a random number. Create a loop that runs until the number is guessed. Ask the user for their guess. If the guessed number equals the actual number then print “Hurraaa you won! GUESSED_NUMBER was the correct guess.” If the guessed number is larger than the actual number then print “Go lower!” If the guessed number is smaller than the actual number then print “Go higher!”
Optional:
Implement lives so the user must guess the number within a limited number of tries. Give the user the chance to play again after a correct guess.


import random

num = random.randrange(0,100)

number_guessed = False

while not number_guessed:
    guess = int(input("Guess a number between 0 and 100: "))
    if guess == num:
        print(f"Hurraaa you won! {guess} was the corret guess.")
        play_again = input("Do you want to play again? (y/n): ")
        if play_again == "y":
            num = random.randrange(0,100)
        else:
            number_guessed = True
    elif guess > num:
        print("Go lower!")
    else:
        print("Go higher!")

title: Week 1: Python Basics I
text:
Project 1: Band Name Generator

The TaskThe Solution


Create a band name generator that processes user input.

Create a welcome message for your program.
Ask the user for the city where they grew up.
Ask the user for the name of their first pet.
Combine the city and pet names and show them their band name.



print("Welcome to the Band Name Generator.")
city = input("What's the name of the city you grew up in?\n")
pet = input("What's your pet's name?\n")
print("Your band name could be " + city + " " + pet)

title: Week 2: Python Basics II
text:
Project 2: Love Calculator

The TaskThe Solution


Everyone knows the love calculators from the internet. You input two names and get a love score. Today you will code your own love calculator. You can be creative and make it all alone with your own creativity or you can use this template:
name1 = your name name2 = their name combined_string = name1 + name2 # convert it to all lowercase
t = number of t’s in combined_string r = number of r’s in combined_string u = … e = … first_digit = t + r + u + e
… second_digit = l + o + v + e
love_score = string representation of first_digit + string representation of second_digit
if the love score is under 10 or greater than 90 then print the score and the phrase “you go together like coke and mentos” if the love score is between inclusive 40 and inclusive 50 then print the score and the phrase “you are mid together” if the love score is 69 then print the score and the phrase “you are like ying and yang” else print the score and the phrase “you are like bonnie and clyde”


print("Welcome to the Love Calculator!")

name1 = input("Input the first name: ")
name2 = input("Input the second name: ")
combined_name = (name1 + name2).lower()

first_digit = combined_name.count("t") + combined_name.count("r") + combined_name.count("u") + combined_name.count("e")
second_digit = combined_name.count("l") + combined_name.count("o") + combined_name.count("v") + combined_name.count("e")
love_score = int(str(first_digit) + str(second_digit))

if love_score < 10 or love_score > 90:
    print(f"Your love score is {love_score}%, you go together like coca cola and mentos.")
elif love_score >= 40 and love_score <= 50:
    print(f"Your love score is {love_score}%, you are mid together.")
elif love_score == 69:
    print(f"Your love score is {love_score}%, you are like ying and yang.")
else:
    print(f"Your love score is {love_score}%, you are like bonnie and clyde.")
